description_long: This object is a metal guard rail commonly found in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or near roads to control pedestrian or vehicle movement. It features a horizontal top rail and a matching bottom rail, both connected by evenly spaced vertical bars, forming a sturdy linear barrier. The frame is constructed from tubular or cylindrical steel, with visible signs of weathering and rust, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The feet of the guard rail are slightly flared outward for stability and rest flat on the ground, suggesting a surface-mounted installation. The ends of the rail are rounded, with no sharp protrusions, enhancing safety for passersby. There are no visible reflective markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its purely functional design.
description: A weathered metal guard rail with vertical bars and rounded ends, designed for urban pedestrian or vehicle control. The structure is sturdy, utilitarian, and surface-mounted with flared feet.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width, vertical bars, and rounded top rail.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and flared feet for stability.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the vertical bars and frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and base structure.
